  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 50,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Extension cords routed across active pedestrian walkways creating trip hazards",
    "Missing guardrails on open-sided floors and elevated work areas",
    "Blocked emergency egress routes and obstructed fire extinguishers",
    "Improvised, unstable scaffolding structures in active use",
    "Unattended and unprotected open floor holes without covers or barricades"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately in the affected areas to address life-safety hazards",
    "Reroute or protect extension cords using heavy-duty cord ramps or suspend them overhead",
    "Install compliant guardrail systems including top rails, mid rails, and toe boards",
    "Clear all obstructions from emergency egress paths and fire extinguisher stations",
    "Dismantle improvised scaffolds and replace with certified, inspected scaffolding systems",
    "Cover and secure all open floor holes with covers marked 'HOLE' or install standard guardrails"
  ],
  "rationale": "The jobsite exhibits multiple critical safety violations that present immediate danger to life and health (IDLH), including fall hazards, fire safety obstructions, and structural instability of improvised access equipment. Immediate corrective action is required to bring the site into compliance with OSHA standards.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause Section 5(a)(1)",
    "29 CFR 1926.501 (Fall Protection)",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart L (Scaffolds)",
    "29 CFR 1926.150 (Fire Protection)",
    "29 CFR 1926.25 (Housekeeping)"
  ]
}
